examples.title: B-Tree Search Algorithm
examples.description: Recursive search algorithm to find a key in a B-Tree
examples.code:
FUNCTION BTreeSearch(x, k)
    i = 1
    WHILE i <= x.n AND k > x.key[i]
        i = i + 1
    END WHILE
    
    IF i <= x.n AND k == x.key[i]
        RETURN (x, i)  // Key found at node x, position i
    END IF
    
    IF x.leaf
        RETURN NIL  // Key not found
    END IF
    
    RETURN BTreeSearch(x.child[i], k)  // Recurse into child
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Starting from root, we find the appropriate child by comparing k with keys. If k matches a key, we return success. If we reach a leaf without finding k, it doesn't exist in the tree.

examples.title: B-Tree Node Class Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of B-Tree node structure with insert functionality
examples.code:
class BTreeNode:
    def __init__(self, t, leaf=False):
        self.t = t  # minimum degree
        self.leaf = leaf
        self.keys = []
        self.children = []
    
    def split_child(self, i):
        """Split full child node at index i"""
        new_node = BTreeNode(self.t, self.leaf)
        old_node = self.children[i]
        
        # Move median key to current node
        median_idx = self.t - 1
        self.keys.insert(i, old_node.keys[median_idx])
        
        # Move second half keys to new node
        new_node.keys = old_node.keys[self.t:]
        old_node.keys = old_node.keys[:self.t-1]
        
        # Move children if not leaf
        if not self.leaf:
            new_node.children = old_node.children[self.t:]
            old_node.children = old_node.children[:self.t]
    
    def insert_non_full(self, k):
        """Insert key into node that is not full"""
        i = len(self.keys) - 1
        
        if self.leaf:
            # Find position and insert
            while i >= 0 and k < self.keys[i]:
                i -= 1
            self.keys.insert(i+1, k)
        else:
            # Find child to descend
            while i >= 0 and k < self.keys[i]:
                i -= 1
            i += 1
            
            # Split child if full
            if len(self.children[i].keys) == 2*self.t - 1:
                self.split_child(i)
                if k > self.keys[i]:
                    i += 1
            
            self.children[i].insert_non_full(k)
examples.explanation: This implementation shows the core B-Tree structure with split operation and non-full insertion. The split operation moves the median key up, and insertion maintains sorted order.

examples.title: B-Tree Insertion in C
examples.description: C implementation showing B-Tree node structure and insert operation
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates the split operation - the heart of B-Tree maintenance. When a child node becomes full (2t-1 keys), it splits into two nodes with t-1 keys each, and the median key moves to the parent.
examples.timeComplexity: O(t) per split, O(log n) total

examples.title: B-Tree Search and Traversal
examples.description: Java implementation showing search and level-order traversal
examples.explanation: This Java implementation shows the B-Tree search algorithm and in-order traversal. The search compares keys sequentially and descends to the appropriate child. Traversal processes left subtree, key, then right subtree recursively.
